vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence. P1 (Yellow Pot) appears empty/soil-only. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor but the Pothos specimen is severely degraded or absent. P2 and P4 are not clearly distinguishable as separate entities; the black pot appears to contain only debris and a single, failing stem.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface. No String of Nickels detected.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: White rabbit anchor present. The Pothos has undergone total senescence or removal.
plant_audit.General: The desk surface shows significant debris (eggshells/fragments) and a complete lack of healthy botanical specimens.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dark and potentially waterlogged or devoid of organic life.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot suggests an amateur intervention (calcium supplementation) that has failed to support plant health.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_T_minus_2: Initial state shows a single, chlorotic, and drooping leaf in the black pot. The white rabbit is present.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_Current: Total collapse. The remaining leaf structure has vanished or withered into the soil. The environment has transitioned to a state of total botanical failure.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of terminal decline. All registered specimens are either dead or have been removed. The environment is no longer supporting the intended flora.
anomalies: Crushed eggshells in the black pot (Intervention)
Total absence of living plant tissue in all pots (Systemic Failure)
vision_report.narrative_description: As a botanical observer, I have performed a chronological audit of the provided image sequence. The initial state showed a struggling Pothos specimen in the black pot alongside a white rabbit anchor. Over the course of the 5-day observation, the plant exhibited progressive postural collapse and chlorosis, eventually leading to a complete disappearance of the specimen. The yellow pot (P1) remained consistently empty throughout the sequence. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment, which has proven ineffective. The current state is one of total botanical loss.
